data.content:
### Sandbox Metrics CLI Command

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/metrics/page.mdx

Command-line interface command to fetch sandbox metrics using the E2B CLI. Requires the sandbox ID as an argument.

```APIDOC
## CLI Command: sandbox metrics

### Description
Fetches and displays performance metrics (CPU, memory, disk usage) for a specified sandbox using the E2B command-line interface.

### Usage
`e2b sandbox metrics <sandbox_id>`

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
- **sandbox_id** (string) - Required - The unique identifier of the sandbox.

### Example Output
```
Metrics for sandbox <sandbox_id>

[2025-07-25 14:05:55Z]  CPU:  8.27% /  2 Cores | Memory:    31 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
[2025-07-25 14:06:00Z]  CPU:   0.5% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
[2025-07-25 14:06:05Z]  CPU:   0.1% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
[2025-07-25 14:06:10Z]  CPU:   0.3% /  2 Cores | Memory:    32 / 484   MiB | Disk:  1445 / 2453  MiB
```
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI - Main Template Command Usage

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.1/template/page.mdx

The base command for managing sandbox templates within the E2B CLI. It serves as an entry point for various template-related operations like build, list, init, and delete.

```bash
e2b template [options] [command]
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox CLI Usage and Options

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Command to create a new sandbox and connect a terminal to it. Allows specifying a template and customizing the execution path or configuration file.

```bash
e2b sandbox create [options] [template]

### Options

 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory `
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. `
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI: Sandbox Metrics

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Displays metrics for a sandbox. Supports following the metrics stream and specifying the output format.

```bash
e2b sandbox metrics [options] <sandboxID>

### Options

 - `-f, --follow: keep streaming metrics until the sandbox is closed `
 - `--format <format>: specify format for printing metrics (json, pretty) [default: pretty]`
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandbox Templates (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.6.0/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates associated with a team. Requires specifying the team ID.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
```

--------------------------------

### Show Sandbox Metrics CLI Usage and Options

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Command to display metrics for a specific sandbox. Supports streaming metrics in real-time and specifying the output format.

```bash
e2b sandbox metrics [options] <sandboxID>

### Options

 - `-f, --follow: keep streaming metrics until the sandbox is closed `
 - `--format <format>: specify format for printing metrics (json, pretty) [default: pretty] `
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI with NPM

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/cli/page.mdx

Installs the E2B CLI globally using NPM. This method is suitable for users who have Node.js and NPM installed. It makes the E2B CLI commands accessible from any directory in your terminal.

```bash
npm i -g @e2b/cli
```

--------------------------------

### Publish Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.6.0/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use. Options allow specifying the path, configuration file, selecting from a list, associating with a team, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - -y, --yes: skip manual publish confirmation 
```

--------------------------------

### Publish Sandbox Template (e2b CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.0/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use. Supports interactive selection and confirmation skipping.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

# Example to publish interactively and skip confirmation:
e2b template publish -s -y
```

--------------------------------

### Kill Sandbox CLI Usage and Options

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Command to terminate one or more sandboxes. Can be used to kill specific sandboxes by ID or all sandboxes, with optional state filtering.

```bash
e2b sandbox kill [options] [sandboxIDs...]

### Options

 - `-a, --all: kill all sandboxes `
 - `-s, --state <state>: when used with -a/--all flag, filter by state, eg. running, paused. Defaults to running `
 - `-m, --metadata <metadata>: when used with -a/--all flag, filter by metadata, eg. key1=value1 `
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.6.0/template/page.mdx

Creates a basic E2B Dockerfile (./e2b.Dockerfile) in the root directory, which can then be used to build a sandbox template. An option is available to change the execution path.

```bash
e2b template init [options]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.2/template/page.mdx

Creates a basic E2B Dockerfile in the root directory to help users start building a sandbox template. It supports specifying the target directory.

```bash
e2b template init [options]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
```

--------------------------------

### Spawn Sandbox CLI Usage and Options

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Command to create a new sandbox and connect a terminal to it. Similar to 'create', it supports specifying a template and customizing the execution path or configuration file.

```bash
e2b sandbox spawn [options] [template]

### Options

 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory `
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. `
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandbox Templates (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.2/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates. It can filter templates by team ID.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandboxes CLI Usage and Options

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Command to list all available sandboxes. Supports filtering by state, metadata, and limiting the number of results. Output format can be specified.

```bash
e2b sandbox list [options]

### Options

 - `-s, --state <state>: filter by state, eg. running, paused. Defaults to running `
 - `-m, --metadata <metadata>: filter by metadata, eg. key1=value1 `
 - `-l, --limit <limit>: limit the number of sandboxes returned `
 - `-f, --format <format>: output format, eg. json, table `
```

--------------------------------

### Authenticate E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/packages/cli/README.md

Logs in to the E2B CLI, establishing an authenticated session. This command is necessary to manage E2B sandboxes and templates. Authentication can also be done via an environment variable for non-interactive sessions.

```bash
e2b auth login
```

```bash
E2B_ACCESS_TOKEN=sk_e2b_... e2b template build
```

--------------------------------

### Publish Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.2/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use by others within a team. Options allow specifying the path, configuration file, selection from a list, team ID, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - `-s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - `-y, --yes: skip manual publish confirmation 
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandbox Templates with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.0/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates. This command can be filtered by a specific team ID.

```bash
e2b template list [options]
```

--------------------------------

### Publish Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.9.1/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use. Options allow specifying the root path, config file, selecting from a list, associating with a team, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>`: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
 - `--config <e2b-toml>`: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
 - `-s, --select`: select sandbox template from interactive list
 - `-t, --team <team-id>`: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
 - `-y, --yes`: skip manual publish confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Run E2B Sandbox CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.1/sandbox/page.mdx

The base command to interact with e2b sandboxes. It accepts various subcommands to manage sandbox lifecycles and retrieve information.

```bash
e2b sandbox [options] [command]
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI using NPM

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/packages/cli/README.md

Installs the E2B CLI tool globally on your system using the Node Package Manager (NPM). This method is suitable for users who have Node.js and NPM installed.

```bash
npm install -g @e2b/cli
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI with Homebrew (macOS)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/cli/page.mdx

Installs the E2B CLI using the Homebrew package manager. This is a straightforward installation for macOS users. It ensures you have the latest stable version of the CLI available in your terminal.

```bash
brew install e2b
```

--------------------------------

### List All Sandboxes (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/cli/list-sandboxes/page.mdx

Lists all currently running sandboxes. This is the basic command to view your sandboxes. No specific dependencies are required beyond the E2B CLI installation.

```bash
e2b sandbox list
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox-template/page.mdx

Installs the E2B command-line interface. This is the first step to manage sandbox templates and interact with the E2B platform.

```bash
brew install e2b
```

```bash
npm i -g @e2b/cli
```

--------------------------------

### Install E2B CLI Beta Version with NPM

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/cli/page.mdx

Installs the beta version of the E2B CLI globally using NPM. This allows you to test the latest features and improvements before they are released in a stable version. Ensure you have Node.js and NPM installed.

```bash
npm i -g @e2b/cli@beta
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.2/template/page.mdx

Builds a sandbox template using a Dockerfile. It supports specifying the Dockerfile path, template name, start command, and build arguments. By default, it looks for e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in the root directory.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - `-d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory. 
 - `-n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores. 
 - `-c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started. 
 - `--ready-cmd <ready-command>: specify command that will need to exit 0 for the template to be ready. 
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - `--cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2. 
 - `--memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512. 
 - `--build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>. 
 - `--no-cache: skip cache when building the template. 
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandbox Templates with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.9.1/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates associated with a specific team. Requires the team ID as an option.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
 - `-t, --team <team-id>`: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
```

--------------------------------

### Specify Ready Command via CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox-template/ready-cmd/page.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to set the ready command using the `e2b template build` CLI command with the `--ready-cmd` option. The command provided will be executed to determine sandbox readiness.

```bash
e2b template build --ready-cmd "<your-ready-command>"
```

--------------------------------

### Publish E2B Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.1/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template. Options include specifying the path, config file, selecting a template interactively, specifying the team ID, and skipping manual confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with.
  -y, --yes: skip manual publish confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### CLI: Spawn e2b sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.8/sandbox/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox and automatically connects the terminal to it. Allows specifying a template and changing the execution path.

```bash
e2b sandbox spawn [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
```

--------------------------------

### List E2B Sandbox Templates with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.1/template/page.mdx

Lists available sandbox templates. Options include specifying the team ID and the output format (e.g., json, pretty).

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with.
  -f, --format <format>: output format, eg. json, pretty
```

--------------------------------

### Unpublish Sandbox Template (e2b CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.0/template/page.mdx

Unpublishes a sandbox template, removing it from public availability. Supports interactive selection and confirmation skipping.

```bash
e2b template unpublish [options] [template]

# Example to unpublish interactively and skip confirmation:
e2b template unpublish -s -y
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize E2B Dockerfile with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.9.1/template/page.mdx

Creates a basic E2B Dockerfile (./e2b.Dockerfile) in the root directory, which can then be used to build a sandbox template. An option is available to specify the target directory.

```bash
e2b template init [options]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>`: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template (e2b CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.0/template/page.mdx

Deletes a sandbox template and its associated configuration file (e2b.toml). It allows for interactive selection and confirmation skipping.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

# Example to delete interactively and skip confirmation:
e2b template delete -s -y
```

--------------------------------

### Publish Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.7.0/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use. Options include specifying the path, configuration file, team ID, and flags to select templates interactively or skip confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - -y, --yes: skip manual publish confirmation
```

--------------------------------

### Delete Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.2/template/page.mdx

Deletes a sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. Options include specifying the path, configuration file, selecting from a list, specifying the team ID, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - `-s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - `-y, --yes: skip manual delete confirmation 
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandbox Templates with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.0.1/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates associated with the specified team ID. This command is useful for managing and reviewing existing templates.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
```

### Migrate E2B Template to SDK Format using E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.3/template/page.mdx

Migrates e2b.Dockerfile and e2b.toml to the new Template SDK format. No specific options are detailed beyond the command itself.

```bash

```

### Publish Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.8.0/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use by others. Options include specifying the path, config file, team ID, and interactively selecting a template or skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  -y, --yes: skip manual publish confirmation
```

### Publish Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.10/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use. Options include specifying the path, selecting from an interactive list, confirming the publish action, and associating with a team ID.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]
```

### List Sandbox Templates (e2b CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.0/template/page.mdx

Lists available sandbox templates associated with a team. Supports specifying the team ID and output format, such as JSON or pretty-printed.

```bash

# Example to list in JSON format:

```

--------------------------------

### List Sandbox Templates with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.1/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates associated with a specific team ID. This command helps in managing and keeping track of existing sandbox templates.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
```

--------------------------------

### Publish Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.0.1/template/page.mdx

Publishes a sandbox template, making it available for use by others within a team or organization. This command allows specifying the template, path, configuration, and team ID, with options to select interactively and skip confirmation.

```bash
e2b template publish [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
 - `-s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
 - `-y, --yes: skip manual publish confirmation
```

### List Sandbox Templates with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.1/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates associated with a specific team. This command helps users to view and manage their existing templates.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
 - `-t, --team <team-id>`: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
```

### Initialize a Basic E2B Dockerfile with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.7.0/template/page.mdx

Creates a basic Dockerfile (e2b.Dockerfile) in the root directory, which can then be used to build a sandbox template. Allows specifying a different root directory for the operation.

```bash
e2b template init [options]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
```

### Login to E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Authenticates the E2B CLI with your E2B account. This command is necessary before you can create or manage custom sandbox templates.

```bash
e2b auth login
```

### Unpublish Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.5.2/template/page.mdx

Unpublishes a sandbox template, revoking its shared availability. Options include specifying the path, configuration file, selecting from a list, team ID, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template unpublish [options] [template]

Options:
 - `-p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - `--config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - `-s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - `-t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - `-y, --yes: skip manual unpublish confirmation 
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize E2B Dockerfile with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.1/template/page.mdx

Creates a basic E2B Dockerfile in the root directory. This serves as a starting point for defining a new sandbox template, which can then be built using the `e2b template build` command.

```bash
e2b template init [options]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
```

--------------------------------

### E2B CLI: List Sandboxes

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v2.2.4/sandbox/page.mdx

Lists all available sandboxes. By default, it only shows running sandboxes. Supports filtering by state, metadata, limiting results, and specifying output format.

```bash
e2b sandbox list [options]

### Options

 - `-s, --state <state>: filter by state, eg. running, paused. Defaults to running `
 - `-m, --metadata <metadata>: filter by metadata, eg. key1=value1 `
 - `-l, --limit <limit>: limit the number of sandboxes returned `
 - `-f, --format <format>: output format, eg. json, pretty `
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.1/template/page.mdx

Builds a sandbox template from a Dockerfile. It supports specifying the Dockerfile path, template name, start command, team ID, and build arguments. It also allows configuration of CPU and memory for the sandbox.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  -d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory.
  -n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores.
  -c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started.
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  --cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2.
  --memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512.
  --build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>.
```

--------------------------------

### List Available Sandbox Templates with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.7.0/template/page.mdx

Lists all available sandbox templates associated with the specified team. Requires a team ID for proper filtering.

```bash
e2b template list [options]

Options:
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
```

--------------------------------

### CLI: View e2b sandbox logs

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.8/sandbox/page.mdx

Retrieves and displays logs for a specific sandbox. Supports filtering by log level, following logs in real-time, and customizing output format.

```bash
e2b sandbox logs [options] <sandboxID>

Options:
  --level <level>: filter logs by level (DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR). The logs with the higher levels will be also shown. [default: INFO]
  -f, --follow: keep streaming logs until the sandbox is closed
  --format <format>: specify format for printing logs (json, pretty) [default: pretty]
  --loggers [loggers]: filter logs by loggers. Specify multiple loggers by separating them with a comma.
```

--------------------------------

### Unpublish Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.6.0/template/page.mdx

Unpublishes a sandbox template, making it unavailable. Options mirror those for publishing, including path specification, config file, selection, team association, and skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template unpublish [options] [template]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list 
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - -y, --yes: skip manual unpublish confirmation 
```

--------------------------------

### Build Sandbox Template (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.6.0/template/page.mdx

Builds a sandbox template from a Dockerfile. It can use './e2b.Dockerfile' or './Dockerfile' by default, or a specified path. This command also generates an 'e2b.toml' configuration file. Options allow specifying the path, Dockerfile, template name, start command, readiness command, team ID, configuration file, CPU count, memory, build arguments, and cache behavior.

```bash
e2b template build [options] [template]

Options:
 - -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory 
 - -d, --dockerfile <file>: specify path to Dockerfile. By default E2B tries to find e2b.Dockerfile or Dockerfile in root directory. 
 - -n, --name <template-name>: specify sandbox template name. You can use the template name to start the sandbox with SDK. The template name must be lowercase and contain only letters, numbers, dashes and underscores. 
 - -c, --cmd <start-command>: specify command that will be executed when the sandbox is started. 
 - --ready-cmd <ready-command>: specify command that will need to exit 0 for the template to be ready. 
 - -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team). 
 - --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory. 
 - --cpu-count <cpu-count>: specify the number of CPUs that will be used to run the sandbox. The default value is 2. 
 - --memory-mb <memory-mb>: specify the amount of memory in megabytes that will be used to run the sandbox. Must be an even number. The default value is 512. 
 - --build-arg <args...>: specify additional build arguments for the build command. The format should be <varname>=<value>. 
 - --no-cache: skip cache when building the template. 
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize E2B Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.0/template/page.mdx

Initializes a new E2B sandbox template by creating a basic e2b.Dockerfile in the root directory. This command helps set up the basic structure for a new template.

```bash
e2b template init [options]
```

### Delete Sandbox Template with E2B CLI

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/cli/v1.0.1/template/page.mdx

Deletes a sandbox template and its associated e2b.toml configuration file. It supports specifying the path, configuration file, and allows for interactive selection or skipping confirmation.

```bash
e2b template delete [options] [template]

Options:
  -p, --path <path>: change root directory where command is executed to <path> directory
  --config <e2b-toml>: specify path to the E2B config toml. By default E2B tries to find ./e2b.toml in root directory.
  -s, --select: select sandbox template from interactive list
  -t, --team <team-id>: specify the team ID that the operation will be associated with. You can find team ID in the team settings in the E2B dashboard (https://e2b.dev/dashboard?tab=team).
  -y, --yes: skip manual delete confirmation
```
